title: Route Remote Access via Managed Access Control Points
description: route remote access via managed access control points

checks.name: Managed Access Control Points are Identified and Implemented
checks.purpose: Identification and implementation of managed access control points such as VPN gateways, jump servers, and bastion hosts is an architectural and organizational GRC documentation concern not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Client

checks.name: Remote Access is Routed Through Managed Network Access Control Points
checks.purpose: Verify that a VPN client is installed and active, direct RDP to external IPs is blocked via Windows Defender Firewall, and default gateway routes traffic through a managed network entry point

checks.name: Managed Access Control Points are Identified and Implemented
checks.purpose: Identification and implementation of managed access control points is an architectural and organizational GRC documentation concern not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Server

checks.name: Remote Access is Routed Through Managed Network Access Control Points
checks.purpose: Verify that inbound RDP and management traffic is restricted to approved internal subnets only, no direct external access is permitted, and IPSec or VPN tunnels are configured for remote management

checks.name: Managed Access Control Points are Identified and Implemented
checks.purpose: Identification and implementation of managed access control points is an architectural and organizational GRC documentation concern not technically verifiable via automated check on Linux

checks.name: Remote Access is Routed Through Managed Network Access Control Points
checks.purpose: Verify that SSH is restricted to internal or VPN subnet source addresses, direct public internet SSH access is blocked via firewall rules, and default route traffic passes through a managed gateway

checks.name: Managed Access Control Points are Identified and Implemented
checks.purpose: Identification and implementation of managed access control points is an architectural and organizational GRC documentation concern not technically verifiable via automated check on Debian

checks.name: Remote Access is Routed Through Managed Network Access Control Points
checks.purpose: Verify that SSH is restricted to internal or VPN subnet source addresses, direct public internet SSH access is blocked via firewall rules, and default route traffic passes through a managed gateway
